# Schedule

::: {.callout-caution title="Program subject to change" appearance="minimal" icon=false}
The program below is tentative and may be updated.
:::

```{r results="asis", echo=FALSE, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(kableExtra)

csv <- "../data/program.csv"
df <- read.csv(csv, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, na.strings = c("", "NA")) |>
  mutate(
    day    = as.integer(day),
    time   = str_trim(time),
    type   = str_trim(type),
    author = str_trim(coalesce(author, "")),
    title  = str_trim(coalesce(title, "")),
    info   = str_trim(coalesce(info, ""))
  ) |>
  arrange(day, time)

# Optional: shorten repeated phrasing
df$author <- gsub("Contributed\\s*from\\s*submitted\\s*abstracts", "Contributed abstracts", df$author)
df$author <- gsub("Contributed\\s*until\\s*the\\s*beginning\\s*of\\s*the\\s*conference", "Contributed pre-conference", df$author)

# Day headers (edit dates if needed)
day_headers <- c(
  "Day 1 — Wed. Sep 17, 2025",
  "Day 2 — Thu. Sep 18, 2025",
  "Day 3 — Fri. Sep 19, 2025"
)

df_out <- df |> select(time, type, author, title)

# Row indices by day (safer than min(which(...)))
idx_by_day <- split(seq_len(nrow(df_out)), df$day)

tbl <- kbl(
  df_out,
  escape = TRUE,
  row.names = FALSE,
  col.names = c("Time", "Type", "Author", "Title")
) |>
  kable_material(full_width = TRUE) |>
  column_spec(1, width = "12%") |>
  column_spec(2, width = "18%") |>
  column_spec(3, width = "28%") |>
  column_spec(4, width = "42%")

# --- Color coding (edit as you like) ---
# Keep these simple “category palettes” like EuroBioC2025.
type_colors <- list(
  Keynote        = "lavender",
  Break          = "lightyellow",
  Lunch          = "lightyellow",
  `Poster session` = "#E8F5E9",
  `Poster pitches` = "#E8F5E9",
  `Conference dinner` = "#FFF3E0",
  `Walking tour` = "#E3F2FD",
  Workshops      = "#F3E5F5",
  `BoF sessions` = "#E0F7FA",
  Opening        = "#F5F5F5",
  Closing        = "#F5F5F5"
)

for (nm in names(type_colors)) {
  ii <- which(df$type == nm)
  if (length(ii) > 0) {
    tbl <- tbl |> row_spec(ii, background = type_colors[[nm]])
  }
}

# Group by day (only if day exists)
for (d in 1:3) {
  if (!is.null(idx_by_day[[as.character(d)]])) {
    tbl <- tbl |> pack_rows(day_headers[d],
                            min(idx_by_day[[as.character(d)]]),
                            max(idx_by_day[[as.character(d)]]))
  }
}

tbl |> cat()
```
